data.title: Asia stocks mostly rise as market sentiment stabilizes on hopes of more tariff exemptions; China market volatile
data.description:
Asia-Pacific stocks mostly higher on Tuesday, tracking overnight gains on Wall Street, where investor sentiment was lifted by President Donald Trump’s decision to exempt key electronic products from new reciprocal tariffs.

However, optimism was tempered by news that the US Commerce Department has launched a national security investigation into semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports, given their strong dependence on Asian, mainly Chinese, supply chains, , keeping global markets on alert.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.88% to above 34,300 while the broader Topix Index added 1.2% to 2,518 on Tuesday, extending gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen slipped past 143 per dollar on Tuesday, retreating from recent highs.

Investors are also closely monitoring upcoming trade negotiations between Washington and Tokyo. Japan’s chief trade negotiator, Akazawa Ryosei, is set to meet with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer later this week.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.23% to around 3,250 while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.7% to 9,815 on Tuesday, with mainland stocks snapping a five-day rally amid persistent concerns about an escalating Sino-US trade war, and the offshore yuan 

Investor sentiment took a hit as Chinese President Xi Jinping warned that "no one wins in a trade war" during the start of his diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia, underscoring Beijing’s cautious stance amid rising geopolitical friction. 

Markets are also assessing the impact of Trump’s reciprocal tariffs on China’s online selling platforms, including PDD’s Temu and Alibaba’s Taobao and TMall.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.14%

Australia (AS51) rose 0.49% to above 7,760 on Tuesday, extending gains from the previous session as firmer underlying prices lifted commodity stocks on the resource-heavy bourse.

RBA's April meeting minutes showed the board kept rates steady at 4.35% in line with expectations, after a 25 basis point cut earlier this year. Meanwhile, financial conditions were seen as somewhat restrictive, and the economic outlook was broadly in line with earlier forecasts.

In the U.S., on Monday, all three major indexes ended higher after a volatile week, as investors welcomed temporary tariff exemptions, including smartphones, computers, and semiconductors, from the newly imposed reciprocal tariffs. President Trump is also reportedly considering pausing his 25% auto tariffs to give carmakers time to adjust their supply chains.

U.S. stock futures edged lower on Tuesday as investors awaited earnings results from major firms, including Johnson & Johnson, Bank of America, Citigroup, Interactive Brokers, and PNC Financial: Dow -0.09%; S&P 500 -0.14%; Nasdaq -0.16%.

Gold rose above $3,220 on Tuesday as uncertainty over President Donald Trump’s tariff plans continued to drive safe-haven demand.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

RBA minutes reveal cautious approach to future interest rate moves

China's March trade surplus above estimates, exports surge while imports fall

Trump intensifies tariff pressure: "No one off the hook," denies tariff rollbacks

Trump says his administration will look at 'whole electronics supply chain'

Japan's industrial output growth revised lower to 2.3% in February

data.title: Whirlpool declares $1.75 dividend
data.description:
• Whirlpool (NYSE:WHR) declares $1.75/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 8.73%
  • Payable June 15; to shareholders of record May 16; ex-div May 16.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $1.75 for fourteen consecutive quarters.
  • See WHR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: RBA minutes reveal cautious approach to future interest rate moves
data.description:
RBA's April meeting minutes showed the board kept rates steady at 4.35% in line with expectations, after a 25 basis point cut earlier this year, emphasizing data reliance and vigilance to risks in determining the timing of future rate changes, despite easing inflation and a tight labor market.

Meanwhile, financial conditions were seen as somewhat restrictive, and the economic outlook was broadly in line with earlier forecasts.

Members highlighted two-sided risks: domestic demand and wages could push inflation higher, while persistent labor market softness or weak consumption could accelerate disinflation.

“Members judged that it was not appropriate at this stage for monetary policy to react to the potential risks that could move outcomes in either direction,” the RBA minutes said. 

Policymakers were seen touting May as an appropriate time to review more changes in monetary policy. 

They also noted that the energy rebate extension announced in the federal budget would impact the profile of headline inflation in 2025 and 2026.

The RBA said that bringing inflation back to its target level remained its biggest priority, and that it wanted to establish that the next decision on rates was not predetermined. 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.2% to above 7,760 on Tuesday, extending gains from the previous session as firmer underlying prices lifted commodity stocks on the resource-heavy bourse.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWA), (NYSEARCA:FXA), (NYSEARCA:FLAU).

More on Australia

  • Australia's trade surplus shrinks to 2020 low; imports surge, exports fall

  • Australia’s monthly CPI eased to three-month low of 2.4% in February

  • Australia's Feb unemployment rate remains unchanged at 4.1%, aligning with expectations

  • Australia’s trade balance exceeds expectations, export growth accelerates and imports decline

  • RBA cuts its cash rate by 25 bps for first time since 2020, in-line with market expectations

data.title: Applied digital outlines $5B data center expansion plans amid strategic shifts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Applied Digital Corporation (APLD) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Wes Cummins highlighted the company's advancements in the High Performance Computing (HPC) Hosting segment, including two major financial transactions: a partnership with Macquarie Asset Management enabling up to $5B in capital for next-generation data centers, and a $375M financing arrangement with Sumitomo Mitsui Bank Corporation. These moves aim to support the development and scalability of the Ellendale campus and other facilities.
  • The Ellendale campus construction remains on track, with the first building expected to start generating revenue in Q4 2025. The second and third buildings are scheduled for completion in 2026 and 2027, respectively.
  • Cummins announced the initiation of strategic options for the Cloud Services Business to address potential customer friction and align with a potential transition to a data center REIT structure.
  • CFO Saidal Mohmand emphasized the significant reduction in the company's cost of capital through strategic financing deals, which include a $450M convertible note and the Macquarie-SMBC arrangements. He stated, "These transactions have not only strengthened our financial position but have also ensured we have the necessary capital to continue funding our data center build-out."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated the Ellendale campus's critical role, with the first building set to come online by the end of 2025. Updates on leasing discussions are expected soon.
  • Cummins suggested that the Cloud Services Business will likely transition to an on-demand model for GPU clusters, potentially increasing revenue opportunities.
  • The company expects continued demand from hyperscalers and significant growth in its HPC data center capacity.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q3 2025 was $52.9M, driven by growth in the Cloud Services Business and additional GPU cluster deployments. The Data Center Hosting segment contributed $35.2M, while the Cloud Services segment generated $17.8M.
  • Cost of revenues reached $49.1M, reflecting operational growth. SG&A expenses decreased to $22.7M due to GPU deployments shifting to revenue-generating activities.
  • Adjusted EBITDA rose to $10M, an 878% increase from the previous quarter, although net loss attributable to common stockholders was $36.1M or $0.16 per share.
  • The company ended the quarter with $261.2M in cash and $689.1M in debt.

Q&A

  • Nick Giles, B. Riley Securities, asked about the Cloud Services Business strategy. Cummins clarified that two of six clusters transitioned to on-demand, with technical issues resolved by March. Cummins confirmed that none of the clusters are in Applied Digital data centers.
  • Thomas Shinske, Cantor Fitzgerald, inquired about the involvement of Macquarie and SMBC. Cummins noted their instrumental role in increasing customer confidence and facilitating leasing discussions.
  • Rob Brown, Lake Street Capital, asked about Ellendale’s remaining steps. CFO Mohmand estimated a $30M-$50M monthly CapEx rate and confirmed timelines for equipment deployment.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the Cloud Services Business sale process and its impact on customer relationships. Cummins acknowledged hyperscaler customers see the Cloud Services Business as a friction point, influencing the decision to evaluate strategic options.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, pressing management on leasing progress and the Cloud Services Business sale. Repeated questions about hyperscaler engagement and pricing indicated skepticism about near-term outcomes.
  • Management maintained confidence, emphasizing strong financial backing and progress in construction timelines. Cummins stated, "We believe our Ellendale campus represents a highly strategic industry asset with significant expansion opportunities."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was greater focus on the Cloud Services Business and hyperscaler customer dynamics, alongside a steady tone regarding the Ellendale campus development.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue declined from $63.9M in Q2 2025 to $52.9M in Q3 2025, attributed to a shift in the Cloud Services Business to on-demand models and technical challenges.
  • Adjusted EBITDA improved significantly, reflecting operational adjustments and cost management.
  • Management's tone shifted to highlight strategic realignments, particularly the potential sale of the Cloud Services Business, compared to a previous focus on expanding partnerships and hyperscaler engagements.
  • Analysts maintained a consistent focus on leasing progress and customer demand, with added scrutiny on the implications of structural changes in the business.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged delays in securing leases for the Ellendale campus, with Cummins admitting the process is taking longer than expected.
  • Analysts raised questions about the potential impact of the Cloud Services Business sale on customer relationships and overall financial performance.
  • Seasonal fluctuations in power costs and technical challenges in transitioning GPU clusters to an on-demand model were identified as operational risks.

Final Takeaway

Applied Digital emphasized its strategic shift toward focusing on its HPC Hosting business, backed by substantial financial commitments from Macquarie and SMBC. While revenue declined sequentially due to operational adjustments in the Cloud Services Business, management highlighted long-term growth potential through infrastructure expansion and leasing opportunities. The company's proactive approach to addressing customer friction and aligning its business model with market demands underscores its commitment to positioning as a leader in the high-performance computing sector.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ADM to stop domestic trading in China, cut jobs in Shanghai
data.description:
Archer Daniels Midland (NYSE:ADM) said Monday it has begun shutting down domestic trading operations in China and laying off staff as part of a global cost-cutting effort announced earlier this year to help it "remain agile in a challenging environment."

The company did not disclose the number of layoffs, but Reuters reported the job cuts would affect 40-50 employees, leaving only ~10 staff in Shanghai.

"The entire Ag Services and Oilseeds team in China has essentially been let go," the source told Reuters, referring to ADM's (NYSE:ADM) largest business segment.

ADM's (ADM) profits have been shrinking due to lower crop prices, inflation-reduced consumer demand and weak crop processing margins, and rising U.S.-China trade tensions are causing new difficulties for the company, which relies on trade between the U.S., the world's leading farm goods exporter, and China, the world's top importer.

ADM (ADM) shares closed +1.3% on Monday after falling last week to their lowest in nearly five years.

data.title: Skillsoft targets $530M-$545M FY2026 revenue amid AI-driven growth initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Skillsoft Corp. (SKIL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ron Hovsepian highlighted that the company delivered revenue exceeding the high end of its guidance range and adjusted EBITDA at the upper end. Efforts under the transformation strategy have positioned Skillsoft for growth in fiscal 2026, with key focuses on operational execution and resource reallocation towards enterprise market segments.
  • Management emphasized major investments in AI-driven technologies, noting that the Skillsoft CAISY AI-powered coach reached over 1 million launches. This tool is being integrated with customer feedback to enhance its functionality, with a broader rollout planned later this fiscal year.
  • New integrations were introduced with platforms such as SAP Talent Intelligence Hub, Oracle, and Docebo, expanding Skillsoft’s ecosystem.
  • CFO Rich Walker noted a significant improvement in adjusted EBITDA, reaching $29.9 million in Q4. He attributed this to disciplined cost management and resource reallocation efforts.

Outlook

  • The company projects FY2026 revenue between $530 million and $545 million, alongside adjusted EBITDA guidance of $112 million to $118 million.
  • Management reaffirmed its commitment to returning to top-line growth and achieving positive free cash flow for FY2026, with anticipated free cash flow between $13 million and $18 million.
  • Investments in transformation initiatives and resource reallocation are expected to drive growth, particularly in the enterprise segment.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q4 2025 was $133.8 million, reflecting a slight year-over-year decline.
  • Talent Development Solutions (TDS) revenue grew 1% year-over-year to $102.8 million, while Global Knowledge revenue declined 13% to $30.9 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA improved to $29.9 million, representing 22% of revenue, driven by expense reductions and improved operational efficiency.
  • The company generated $13.2 million in free cash flow during the quarter, a year-over-year increase of $7.8 million.

Q&A

  • Ken Wong, Oppenheimer: Questioned the impact of macroeconomic uncertainty on business operations. CEO Hovsepian explained that the company is monitoring potential shifts in customer decision-making, emphasizing preparedness through platform adaptability and operational strategies.
  • Mr. Wong inquired about the large deal activity. Management highlighted notable wins, such as a $22 million total contract value from TDS’s top 10 deals, demonstrating strong enterprise engagement.
  • Questions also focused on the CAISY AI tool and its adoption. CEO Hovsepian noted that customer feedback during the preview phase indicated high engagement, with a significant portion of participants utilizing professional services to create customized simulations.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with inquiries focusing on potential market risks and the durability of recent improvements in metrics such as dollar retention rate (DRR). The tone was neutral to slightly positive.
  • Management demonstrated confidence during both prepared remarks and analyst exchanges, emphasizing progress on transformation goals and strategic investments. Statements like "we feel very good about that" highlighted a confident outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained consistently focused on growth and operational improvements, while analysts remained slightly skeptical about macroeconomic impacts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY2026 reflects a targeted revenue range of $530 million to $545 million, compared to the previous year’s $531 million actual revenue.
  • Dollar retention rate improved to 105% in Q4 compared to 98% in Q3, driven by improved customer success and product enhancements.
  • Management maintained consistent focus on AI-driven initiatives and enterprise market penetration, building on prior-quarter strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainties, including potential impacts from evolving government policies, as areas requiring close monitoring.
  • Challenges in the Global Knowledge segment persisted, with year-over-year revenue declines still a concern despite stabilization efforts.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential elongation of decision-making cycles among customers due to broader market uncertainties.

Final Takeaway

Skillsoft Corp. emphasized its strategic focus on AI-driven growth, enterprise market penetration, and operational efficiency as key drivers for FY2026. With revenue guidance set at $530 million to $545 million and positive free cash flow targets, management remains confident in achieving a growth trajectory supported by robust product innovation and customer engagement. However, macroeconomic uncertainties and ongoing challenges in certain business segments remain areas to watch.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. natural gas slumps to nine-week low on record production, expected lower demand
data.description:
U.S. natural gas futures fell to nine-week lows Monday following record production over the weekend and forecasts for less demand next week than previously expected.

Financial firm LSEG said average gas output in the Lower 48 U.S. states rose to 106.3B cf/day so far in April, up from a monthly record of 106.2B cf/day in March, and hit a record 107.4B cf/day on Saturday and Sunday, exceeding the previous all-time high of 107.3B cf/day on March 24.

Looking ahead, however, analysts say energy firms could cut back on oil drilling in coming weeks because of falling crude futures related in part to uncertainty tied to President Trump's on-again off-again tariffs, as any reduction in oil drilling could boost gas prices by cutting gas output associated with that production.

Front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for May delivery finished -5.7% to $3.325/MMBtu, its sixth loss in the past seven sessions and lowest settlement since February 7.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Meteorologists forecast temperatures in the Lower 48 states would remain mostly warmer than normal through the end of April.

"The pattern for late April into May favors light demand as heating degree days slowly fade as the northern U.S. warms, while cooling degree days gradually increase as highs of 80s and 90s gain ground across the southern and central U.S.," NatGasWeather.com wrote.

On the bullish side is continued high LNG feedgas demand, but stronger Y/Y wind and solar power generation is bearish for gas, the forecaster added, "and with strong winds across many regions, renewables will be quite strong this week."

data.title: U.S. shale production likely to slow due to oil price weakness, UBS says
data.description:
Crude oil futures eked out slim gains Monday, as the Trump administration's temporary relief from tariffs on electronic goods was offset by a 150K bbl/day cut in OPEC's demand growth outlook, underscoring the market's troubled outlook and high level of uncertainty.

Oil prices backed off during the day as equities trimmed some gains, BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler said, believing the oil market "lost its luster with the selloff in equities, and there's still a lot of demand concerns in the market because there are still a lot of what ifs and uneasiness over the tariffs."

Oil appears to have stabilized following Trump's tariff rollout, but at levels that underscore the market's bearish view on demand, according to John Coleman of Sparta Commodities.

"Trade war frictions continue to weigh on WTI, as retaliatory tariffs and likely more policy uncertainty to come force it to price far more competitively than usual," Coleman wrote, adding that WTI last week appeared to offer the best relative value globally, but the U.S. benchmark lost some of its competitive edge with a softening in North Sea prices and a near-collapse of Middle Eastern premium.

OPEC cut its global economic growth expectations, saying in its monthly oil market report that it now forecasts oil demand to grow by 1.3M bbl/day this year, below its previous outlook for 1.45M bbl/day, and lowered its forecasts for world economic growth this year and next, citing U.S. trade tariffs.

OPEC's oil demand view is still at the higher end of industry forecasts and it expects oil use will continnue to climb for years, unlike the International Energy Agency, which sees demand peaking this decade; its report is due out on Tuesday.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery closed up $0.03 to $61.53/bbl, and front-month June Brent crude (CO1:COM) ended +0.2% to $64.88/bbl, still the fifth lowest settlement value this year for both benchmarks.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

U.S. shale producers likely will further slow down their activity as oil prices seem set to remain below $65/bbl, according to UBS, noting the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas Energy Survey indicates shale companies need a $65/bbl average to profitably drill a new well.

"Some indicators... suggest that the oil market remains tight, but tariffs together with the ongoing uncertainty over the U.S. administration's next steps are likely to weigh on economic growth," UBS strategist Giovanni Staunovo wrote, as the bank lowered its global oil demand growth forecast for this year by 400K bbl/day to 800K bbl/day, and now expects an oversupply of 400K bbl/day from 100K bbl/day previously.

Goldman Sachs now sees oil demand rising by only 300K bbl/day between the end of last year and the end of 2025, and cut its global demand growth forecasts for the end of 2026 by 900K bbl/day, with large surpluses of 800K bbl/day in 2025 and 1.4M bbl/day in 2026 continuing to exert downward pressure on oil prices.

The bank forecasts Brent and WTI oil prices to tilt lower, averaging $63/bbl and $59/bbl, respectively, for the rest of 2025, and $58/bbl and $55/bbl, respectively, in 2026.

J.P. Morgan also lowered its oil price forecasts for 2025 and 2026, citing higher production from OPEC+ and weaker demand; the bank now sees 2025 Brent price at $66/bbl compared to its previous outlook for $73/bbl and drops its 2026 target to $58/bbl from $61/bbl, while reducing its 2025 WTI price guidance to $62/bbl from $69/bbl and its 2026 view to $53/bbl from $57/bbl.

data.title: TOMI Environmental projects $1.4M CES deals in 2025 while expanding global partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TOMI Environmental Solutions (TOMZ) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Halden Shane discussed the company's progress in 2024 and early successes in 2025, emphasizing efforts to expand the SteraMist iHP brand globally and secure valuable partnerships in key markets. He highlighted the launch of the SteraMist Integrated System (SIS) and its strong market reception, with a pipeline of opportunities exceeding $1 million since its October 2024 launch.
  • The company has been actively working on seven custom-engineered system (CES) projects, securing its first CES deal for 2025 valued at $3.7 million collectively across all CES and hybrid projects. Discussions are ongoing for 46 additional projects, with plans to close five deals in 2025, estimated at $1.4 million.
  • CFO Nick Jennings confirmed operational cost reductions in 2024, including a 41% cut in R&D expenses and a 20% reduction in consulting expenses. He also noted a targeted effort to return to sustainable positive cash flow.
  • COO E.J. Shane outlined the growth of the SIS platform, including contracts worth $575,000 for three SIS offerings in 2025, as well as the company's successful partnership with PBSC Biotech and six other regional partners in markets like Malaysia, India, and Italy.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates significant revenue contributions in 2025 from SIS and CES projects, complemented by strengthened distribution channels and independent representatives.
  • The company aims to advance its razor/razor-blade business model, with BIT Solution sales showing a 200% year-to-date increase compared to the same period last year.
  • Guidance for ongoing CES and SIS sales suggests a focus on long-term pipeline management, with estimated revenue recognition extending beyond 2025.
  • Plans include exploring partnerships in countries like Panama, Nigeria, Israel, and Australia, further expanding TOMI's global footprint.

Financial Results

  • The company reported $7.7 million in GAAP revenue for 2024, up from $7.4 million in 2023, with a non-GAAP revenue figure of $8 million reflecting a 6% increase. Mobile equipment sales rose 23% year-over-year, while BIT Solution sales grew 9%.
  • GAAP gross margins stood at 46%, down from 58% due to increased inventory reserves. Non-GAAP gross margins were reported at 60%, a slight improvement from 59% in 2023.
  • GAAP operating loss increased to $4.1 million from $3.3 million year-over-year, while non-GAAP operating loss reduced to $1.7 million from $2.8 million.
  • Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024, were approximately $665,000, with a working capital of $3.8 million.

Q&A

  • Todd Felte from StoneX Wealth Management inquired about guidance for 2025 revenues and margins. CEO Shane stated margins are expected to remain consistent. COO Shane highlighted the growth of the SIS and CES pipelines, projecting ongoing deliveries and servicing into 2026.
  • Felte asked about the potential for improved margins through the razor/razor-blade model. COO Shane confirmed an increasing mix of BIT Solution sales, with a balance between gallon and drum offerings.
  • John Nelson, a private investor, questioned whether tariffs would impact operations. CEO Shane noted no current issues but acknowledged the fluidity of the situation. Nelson also asked about cash adequacy, to which Shane responded that a convertible note raise of $3-$3.5 million is planned to address opportunities.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were cautiously optimistic, with questions focusing on future revenue visibility, margin improvements, and growth potential in key markets like food safety and government contracts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strategic partnerships and pipeline growth while addressing challenges like returned orders and financial sustainability.
  • Compared to Q3 2024, the tone was slightly more forward-looking, with greater emphasis on partnerships and expanding market opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for CES and SIS sales in 2025 reflects an expanded focus compared to Q3 2024, with higher projected revenue contributions.
  • Management's tone showed increased confidence in the SIS platform and its market adoption, contrasting with Q3's emphasis on immediate profitability.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted from operational efficiency to long-term growth strategies and scalability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted challenges in cash flow for 2024 but expects improvement in 2025 with better execution and progress payments.
  • The company is monitoring potential tariff impacts and changes in regulatory environments for key markets like food safety and medical devices.
  • Analysts raised concerns about returned orders and the company's ability to scale operations with limited cash reserves.

Final Takeaway

TOMI Environmental Solutions is leveraging its expanding SIS and CES platforms to drive growth in 2025, supported by global partnerships and a robust project pipeline. With a focus on operational efficiency and strategic market expansion, the company aims to enhance revenue visibility and scale its razor/razor-blade model. While challenges remain, particularly in cash flow and regulatory environments, management's forward-looking strategy and strong market positioning provide a solid foundation for future growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ametek rated Equal-weight in new coverage at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Ametek (NYSE:AME) on Monday was rated Equal-weight in new research coverage by analysts at financial-services firm Morgan Stanley. They said the supplier of precision electronics has a durable business model, but the consensus estimate for organic growth may be too optimistic.

Ametek (NYSE:AME) is driven by a “customer-centric flywheel,” whereby early customer feedback informs internal R&D and acquisition decisions, Christopher Snyder, analyst at Morgan Stanley, said in an April 14 report. These innovations, once integrated, are scaled across the company's portfolio of more than 40 distinct business lines, fueling continuous growth.

The model gives Ametek (AME) strong pricing power and resilience in downturns, making it particularly well-positioned as macro growth concerns escalate and new tariffs loom, Snyder noted, referencing the potential impact of renewed U.S. trade restrictions.

Ametek's (AME) industrial brands include Muirhead Avionics, Precitech, Drexelbrook, Dunkermotoren, Gatan, Spectro, Solarton Metrology and Atlas Material Testing Solutions.

Margin strength

While organic growth remains a point of concern, Snyder praised Ametek’s (AME) track record of margin expansion and operational discipline. Since 2019, the company has grown margins by 65 basis points a year, even in the absence of meaningful organic volume growth. This performance, bolstered by consistent and accretive mergers and acquisitions, has led to a compound annual growth rate of 10% for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization — outpacing peers.

“It is rare and impressive to expand margins through a period of imbalanced M&A versus organic growth,” Snyder wrote.

This margin resilience is expected to help Ametek (AME) defend earnings in the face of Trump 2.0 tariffs, making it a key driver for near-term estimate revisions.

Valuation upside if execution delivers

Morgan Stanley values Ametek (AME) at roughly 23 times forward earnings per share for 2026–2027, representing a 20% premium to the Standard & Poor’s 500 (SP500) stock index and a 15% premium to industry peers. The bank set a price target of $170 a share for Ametek (AME).

This premium is justified, Snyder said, by the firm’s M&A-driven growth algorithm, which targets 8% to 10% top-line growth, split evenly between acquisitions and organic initiatives.

However, he warned that consensus estimates of 5% organic growth from 2025–2027 may be too aggressive, especially given Ametek’s (AME) heavy international exposure (around 50% of revenue), which could be negatively affected by U.S. protectionism.

“Consensus is setting a high bar on organic growth,” Snyder said, pointing to his team’s estimate of more modest organic growth of about 4%.

What could change rating

Morgan Stanley would consider upgrading Ametek (AME) if signs of improved order momentum and a stronger book-to-bill ratio emerge — indicators that the company could meet or exceed organic growth targets. Conversely, a breakdown in the M&A pipeline or unexpected margin pressure could prompt a more bearish outlook.

Despite current concerns, Snyder emphasized that Ametek (AME) remains a high-quality industrial name with significant compounding potential, so long as it can maintain its execution edge and capitalize on acquisition opportunities.
